topic: My HOA newsletter has been listing my house with a different family's name for six issues straight
title: My HOA Newsletter Has Been Listing My House With a Different Family's Name | True Scary Story

scenes.narration: My HOA newsletter has listed my house with a different family's name for six issues straight.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a folded printed HOA newsletter on a kitchen counter, the front page visible with a list of resident names, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Suburban development. Two hundred homes. Newsletter goes to every address quarterly.
scenes.image_prompt: wide aerial shot of a suburban housing development at dusk, identical houses arranged on curved streets, no movement, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I am the only resident at thirteen Bellrose Court.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a brick mailbox with the address 13 BELLROSE COURT in metal numbers, dim afternoon light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: First saw it in last March's issue. New Resident Welcome. Thirteen Bellrose. The Hawthornes.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of an HOA newsletter page showing a New Resident Welcome section with a name and address visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Called the HOA office. They said I am the Hawthornes.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a phone screen showing an outgoing call to an HOA office number, dim ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: My name on file has been the Hawthornes since I moved in. I have proof of purchase.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a real estate closing document on a wooden desk, a name highlighted in yellow marker, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Drove to county records. Asked them.
scenes.image_prompt: wide interior of a county records office with a clerk seen from behind at a counter, no face visible, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: My deed lists me correctly. The county knows my name. The HOA does not.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of a printed property deed with a notarized signature visible at the bottom of the page, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Each newsletter mentions the Hawthornes more. Birthday wishes. A funeral notice. A new child.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a stack of HOA newsletters on a kitchen counter, headlines and names visible, dim ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: Last issue's photograph showed my front porch. A family of four standing on it.
scenes.image_prompt: close detail of an HOA newsletter page with a printed photograph of a suburban front porch and four figures visible, no faces in focus, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: I was inside. I saw nothing from my window.
scenes.image_prompt: close shot of a hand holding back a curtain at a front window, only the hand visible, dim ambient light, vertical 9:16 framing

scenes.narration: The neighbors wave at the Hawthornes now. Not at me.
scenes.image_prompt: wide shot of two suburban neighbors waving across a fence at a closed front door, seen from behind, no faces visible, vertical 9:16 framing
